title: Spicy Tunisian Bread Galette
description: A twist on traditional Tunisian bread, this crumbly galette is perfect for a sick day. The combination of spices and herbs adds a depth of flavor that's sure to satisfy.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine 2 cups of bread flour and 1 teaspoon of salt.
Add 1 teaspoon of sugar and 1 teaspoon of active dry yeast to the flour mixture.
Gradually add 1 cup of warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 2 equal pieces.
Roll out each piece into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place one of the dough circles in the preheated earthenware cook and top it with 1/4 cup of chopped fresh herbs (such as parsley, cilantro, or rosemary).
Drizzle the top of the galette with 1 tablespoon of olive oil and sprinkle with 1/4 teaspoon of cayenne pepper.
Bake the galette for 15 minutes or until it's golden brown and crispy.
Repeat the process with the second piece of dough and top it with 1/4 cup of chopped fresh herbs and 1/4 teaspoon of cayenne pepper.
Bake the second galette for an additional 10-15 minutes or until it's golden brown and crispy.
Remove the galettes from the earthenware cook and let them cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes before serving.
Slice the galettes into wedges and serve warm.
Note: Traditionally, this dish is cooked in a vessel with a lazy-susan-spinner, which allows for easy serving and turning of the galettes.
